heading: Machine-readable, tamper-resistant entry and exit documents
label: 1732 Machine-readable, tamper-resistant entry and exit documents
notes: Amendments2015—Subsec. (c). struck out subsec. (c), which related to technology standard for visa waiver participants.Pub. L. 114–1132004—Subsecs. (b)(2)(A), (c)(1), (2). substituted “2005” for “2004”.Pub. L. 108–299

Abolition of Immigration and Naturalization Service and Transfer of FunctionsFor abolition of Immigration and Naturalization Service, transfer of functions, and treatment of related references, see note set out under .section 1551 of this title
amendments: Amendments2015—Subsec. (c). struck out subsec. (c), which related to technology standard for visa waiver participants.Pub. L. 114–1132004—Subsecs. (b)(2)(A), (c)(1), (2). substituted “2005” for “2004”.Pub. L. 108–299

children.label: A
children.text: implementation of the requirements of subsections (b) and (c); and

children.label: B
children.text: deployment of the equipment and software to allow biometric comparison and authentication of the documents described in subsections (b) and (c).

children.heading: Estimates
children.label: 2 Estimates
children.text: In addition to the assessment required by paragraph (1), the report required by that paragraph shall include an estimate of the costs to be incurred, and the personnel, man-hours, and other support required, by the Department of Justice, the Department of State, and NIST to achieve the objectives of subparagraphs (A) and (B) of paragraph (1).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than , the Attorney General and the Secretary of State shall issue to aliens only machine-readable, tamper-resistant visas and other travel and entry documents that use biometric identifiers. The Attorney General and the Secretary of State shall jointly establish document authentication standards and biometric identifiers standards to be employed on such visas and other travel and entry documents from among those biometric identifiers recognized by domestic and international standards organizations.October 26, 2004

children.heading: Readers and scanners at ports of entry
children.label: 2 Readers and scanners at ports of entry

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than , the Attorney General, in consultation with the Secretary of State, shall install at all ports of entry of the United States equipment and software to allow biometric comparison and authentication of all United States visas and other travel and entry documents issued to aliens, and passports issued pursuant to subsection (c)(1).October 26, 2005

children.heading: Use of readers and scanners
children.label: B Use of readers and scanners
i
children.text: domestic and international standards organizations determine to be highly accurate when used to verify identity;

children.label: ii
children.text: can read the biometric identifiers utilized under subsections (b)(1) and (c)(1); and

children.label: iii
children.text: can authenticate the document presented to verify identity.

children.heading: Use of technology standard
children.label: 3 Use of technology standard
children.text: The systems employed to implement paragraphs (1) and (2) shall utilize the technology standard established pursuant to .section 1379 of this title

children.heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 114–113, div. O, title II, § 202(c)129 Stat. 2989Dec. 18, 2015
children.label: c Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 114–113, div. O, title II, § 202(c)129 Stat. 2989Dec. 18, 2015

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary to carry out this section, including reimbursement to international and domestic standards organizations.
